title: Ganne
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Conflicting|''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''|"[[End Game]]"}} {{Character |type=Bureaucrat |image=[[File:GenericBossIcon-SWGB.png]] |name=Ganne |homeworld=[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' |birth= |death=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Naboo''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of "[[End Game]]," which include Ganne's [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. |species=[[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Yousa needen to be knowen desa ways to Otoh Gunga.''"
"''Tell me.''"
"''Mesa tell yousa. Yousa take yous mackineeks through da Rellias Straits.|Ganne and Maul, with the former mind tricked by the latter|{{StoryCite|story=End Game|book=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|bformatted=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, 2012 edition}} }} '''Ganne''' was an [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] who served as the [[Boss/Legends|boss]] of the [[City/Legends|city]] [[Rellias]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], during the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], Ganne was sought out by the Federation-aligned [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] so that the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] could [[Siege of Otoh Gunga|attack]] [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]], the Gungan [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. Maul and half a [[Company/Legends|company]] of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] traversed through the [[Gungan Swamp]] to find Rellias, and instead they encountered an ambush from Ganne's forces. The droids defeated the Gungans, and Ganne was personally [[Prisoner/Legends|captured]] by Maul, who [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricked]] the boss into guiding them through the [[Gates of Rellias]] to Otoh Gunga. He attempted to resist Maul's [[Force power/Legends|use]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] but was unable to do so. On the journey through the [[Rellias Straits]] via an [[S-DST]], an [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]], the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] resorted to tying Ganne to the bow as a figurehead to prevent Gungan attacks from the shorelines. At the capital, Maul realized that the Gungans had already evacuated the city, so he planned to mind trick Ganne into revealing where their leader, Boss [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]], had gone. However, the Ankura Gungan was able to resist. Maul therefore used his [[Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] Ganne into revealing that Nass was at the [[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Gungan Sacred Place]], resulting in Ganne's [[Death/Legends|death]]. ==Biography== ===Invasion of Naboo=== {{Quote|The name of the governor of Rellias.''"
"''Boss… Boss Ganne. An Ankura Gungan—the ones with green skin and hooded eyes.|Leika and Darth Maul|}} Ganne was an [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Ganne was the [[Boss/Legends|boss]] of the [[Water/Legends|underwater]] [[City/Legends|city]] [[Rellias]]. Following the Trade Federation's [[Capture of Theed/Legends|conquest]] of Naboo's [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], the company's leadership turned its attention to defeating the Gungans, fearful of the threat they posed. The Trade Federation had difficulty locating the Gungans until a [[Prisoner/Legends|captured]] [[Unidentified mariner|mariner]] from the city [[Harte Secur]] told the [[B1-series battle droid|B1 battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] about Ganne and Rellias. The [[Battle droid/Legends|droids]] were, however, unable to locate Rellias until their ally, the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], learned Rellias's location and Ganne's name from the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] surveyor [[Leika]]. [[File:Maul_on_Patrol.png|thumb|right|300px|Darth Maul and several battle droids search for Ganne.]] Maul ordered a patrol of [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|Single Trooper Aerial Platforms (STAPs)]] to [[Scout/Legends|scout]] the [[Gungan Swamp]], and he, alongside OOM-9, traveled through a channel to Rellias aboard an [[S-DST]]—a type of aquatic [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]]—with half a [[Company/Legends|company]] of B1 battle droids and a contingent of [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]]. At a set of stone fortifications, Gungans under Ganne's command waited for the [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. When they arrived, Ganne launched a multi-pronged [[Siege of Otoh Gunga|attack]]. [[Militiagung/Legends|Militiagungs]] fired projectile [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] at the STAPs, bongo [[Submarine/Legends|submersibles]] emerged from the channel to attack the destroyer, and [[Catapult/Legends|catapults]] strapped to [[Fambaa/Legends|fambaas]] fired [[Booma/Legends|boomas]] at the droids. The S-DST beached itself to allow the droids aboard to disembark, where Ganne led a [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] cavalry charge against them alongside an [[Unidentified Gungan general|Ankura Gungan general]]. ===Capture=== {{Quote|You’ll open the gates when we arrive.''"
"''Mesa open dissa gates when wesa riven.|Maul and Ganne, with the latter mind tricked by the former|}} [[File:Militiagung-SWCTP.png|thumb|left|200px|Ganne led a kaadu cavalry (one pictured) charge against the Trade Federation.]] Maul leapt from the destroyer and into the air where he clung to the footrest of a STAP. At a distance twenty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] from Ganne, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Force power/Legends|used]] [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Force jump/Legends|launch]] himself through a score of mounted Gungans, tearing them apart with his [[Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. When he was five meters from the boss, Maul leapt again, [[Death/Legends|decapitating]] the [[General/Legends|general]] with his [[Sith lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and pulling Ganne from his mount with his left [[Limb/Legends|hand]]. The boss rose to his feet and scrambled to retrieve his [[Electropole/Legends|electropole]], but Maul knocked it away, grabbed Ganne by the [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and pulled him to a [[Tree/Legends|treeline]] away from the battle. The boss's [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were rolling around and he began to drool. Maul initially brought the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] closer to Ganne's face, but decided to deactivate it so that he could [[Mind trick/Legends|mind trick]] him instead. Maul asked Ganne for the direction to [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]], the Gungan capital, and the mind-tricked boss responded that they needed to take the route through the [[Rellias Straits]]. Maul yanked the boss's ears and ordered him to open the [[Gates of Rellias]] for the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] as they arrived. Maul was satisfied with his successful manipulation of Ganne as he dragged the boss to his feet and to the S-DST. Maul and the remaining droids used the destroyer to navigate through the Rellias Straits. At the gates, Ganne attempted to resist betraying his people, but he relented after further Force use from Maul. The droids proceeded through gates through to [[Lake Paonga/Legends|Lake Paonga]], which contained Otoh Gunga under its surface. ===Death=== {{Quote|Open the gate, Gungan.''"
"''No! Mesa no betrayin' Otoh Gunga!''"
"''You will open the gate.''"
[hypnotized] "''Mesa… open gate.|Darth Maul and Ganne at the Gates of Rellias|audio=MaulControlsGanne.flac|}} [[File:Rellias_channel.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Under Maul's control, Ganne led the Trade Federation to the Rellias Straits (entrance pictured).]] Groups of militiagungs appeared on the lake's shoreline and fired at the Trade Federation until Maul secured Ganne to the bow of the S-DST. The Gungans ceased their attacks for the remainder of the destroyer's passage to Otoh Gunga and only brandished their electropoles while screaming war cries. As OOM-9 ordered [[depth charge]]s detonated throughout the lake and sent submersibles to investigate the city, Maul realized that Otoh Gunga's residents had heard of the fall of Rellias and evacuated. Determined to find the location of Otoh Gunga's boss, [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]], Maul stormed to retrieve Ganne from the bow and interrogated him, but the boss was able to resist the Sith's mind tricks. Maul snorted and thought that Ganne was not as primitive as he had expected. He changed tactics, igniting his lightsaber to begin [[Torture/Legends|torturing]] Ganne, who slowly revealed that Nass was at the [[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Gungan Sacred Place]]. Maul felt that Ganne's resistance had showed honor, and once the boss had died, Maul rolled his blistered corpse into the water. OOM-9 waited until after Maul was finished with the Gungan to tell him that [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] needed to inform Maul that the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Human]] [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen of Naboo]], [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], had returned to the planet. The Sith apprentice later used the information gained from Ganne to [[Spy/Legends|spy]] on the Gungans after they formed an alliance with the [[Queen/Legends|queen]]. ==Personality and traits== An Ankura Gungan with [[Color/Legends|green]] skin, Boss Ganne served as the leader of Rellias and [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] Gungan forces during the Siege of Otoh Gunga. He was weak-willed enough to be susceptible to Maul's mind trick, coming under the Sith apprentice's control. He attempted to resist after initially succumbing, and in Maul's estimation, demonstrated honor through his resistance. The boss revealed the Gungan's secret location under torture but only did so slowly and after enduring significant pain. ==Skills and abilities== Ganne was able to lead his people into battle and was agile in spite of his girth. ==Equipment== During the Siege of Otoh Gunga, Ganne rode a kaadu, which he used to lead a cavalry charge. He carried a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] which he could use as a weapon and wielded an electropole during the cavalry charge. Ganne wore brown [[Robe/Legends|robes]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Acck! Yousa gotten me! What isa you? Some kind of devil thing?|Boss Ganne's reaction to Darth Maul in ''Galactic Battlegrounds''|audio=MaulDevil.flac|}} Boss Ganne was created for the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', released on [[November 11]], 2001,{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}} in which he was voiced by [[Neil Kaplan]]. The character was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|Prima strategy guide]] by [[Steven L. Kent]],[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] released on [[November 7]], 2001.{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Galactic-Battlegrounds-Official/dp/0761537503|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}} Ganne appears in "The Search for an Underwater Kingdom," the fifth level of the Trade Federation campaign. The character can also be included in player-made custom scenarios, and his name can appear as the leader of an enemy faction in the standard game mode. The events of "The Search for an Underwater Kingdom" were adapted in the short story "[[End Game]]" by [[James Luceno]], published in the [[2012]] re-release of the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|novelization]] of the [[1999]]{{Film|I}} [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. The short story portrayed significant differences in the events concerning Ganne compared to those in the video game, in which the entire sequence involving Ganne takes place on land, rather than aboard an S-DST in a river channel as portrayed in "End Game." This article primarily follows the short story's events, with contradicting events from ''Galactic Battlegrounds'' and the Prima guide detailed below. [[File:Maul_Ganne.png|thumb|right|250px|Darth Maul approaches Ganne in ''Galactic Battlegrounds''.]] In "The Search for an Underwater Kingdom," the player is tasked with finding Ganne so that he can lead the Trade Federation to Rellias, and therefore Otoh Gunga. Ganne rides a kaadu and resides behind a [[command center]] in a [[Ganne's compound|compound]] north of Rellias, defended by militiagungs, kaadu troopers, and catapaults. The player's forces, led by Darth Maul, are required to break through the Gungan defenses and destroy the command center, which the Prima guide recommends the player use [[Energy pummel/Legends|energy pummels]] to do. The player has to send Maul to approach Ganne, who is shocked by the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak's]] [[Demon/Legends|devilish]] appearance. Maul mind tricks Ganne into telling him how the droids can access Otoh Gunga, with the boss revealing that they need to access the Gates of Rellias to the southwest. Ganne accompanies the Trade Federation forces to Rellias and can even be made to fight the Gungans beside them. Once they reach the Gates of Rellias, Ganne attempts to resist Maul's command to open them but is unable to do so. The Trade Federation forces defeat the Gungans at Rellias and reach the entrance to Lake Paonga. The Prima guide notes that the only real way that the player can lose the level is if Ganne is killed. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' {{1st}} *{{StoryCite|story=End Game|book=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|bformatted=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, 2012 edition}} *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Ganne|soundcat=Audio files of Ganne}} *[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Ankura Gungans]] [[Category:Bosses]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Naboo residents]]
